claim_boundary.safe_claim: Quinn has source/API evidence plus a local endpoint-rebind runtime PASS: the rebind_recv test passed, connected/got-conn/rebound logs appeared, the proto migration test passed, migration was initiated, PATH_CHALLENGE/PATH_RESPONSE counters were nonzero, and the new path was validated.
claim_boundary.unsafe_claim: Quinn browser handover, HTTP/3 application continuity, managed deployment continuity, or quic-go-equivalent per-connection AddPath/Probe/Switch control.
claim_boundary.next_gap: Use this as a Rust-stack endpoint-rebind runtime positive control; only build a custom Quinn HTTP/3 workload if reviewers require application-layer Rust evidence.

evidence.observation: Endpoint::rebind switches a Quinn endpoint to a new UDP socket.
evidence.implication: Quinn has a public runtime control surface for endpoint-wide local address changes.

evidence.observation: rebind_abstract updates the endpoint address live and affects all active connections.
evidence.implication: Quinn's active local-address control is endpoint-wide rather than per-connection AddPath/Probe/Switch.

evidence.observation: The rebind_recv Tokio test connects client/server endpoints, rebinds the client UDP socket, and reads a server unidirectional stream.
evidence.implication: A simple Quinn stream workload can complete after endpoint rebind in the upstream test.

evidence.observation: The migration test changes the client address, sends data, and observes the server remote address update.
evidence.implication: Quinn has protocol-level migration/rebinding evidence beyond the endpoint runtime test.

evidence.observation: Outgoing packets on an unvalidated path include PATH_CHALLENGE and update frame statistics.
evidence.implication: Runtime claims can be tied to path-validation frame evidence rather than only test exit code.

evidence.observation: Queued path responses are emitted and counted in frame statistics.
The study records cargo test -p quinn-proto migration and cargo test -p quinn rebind as passing at the audited commit.
evidence.implication: The runtime packet deepens an existing Quinn row with a dedicated fail-closed artifact.
